+#!/bin/sh -e
+
+#
+# Copyright (c) 2026 Oracle and/or its affiliates.
+#
+# The Universal Permissive License (UPL), Version 1.0
+#
+# Subject to the condition set forth below, permission is hereby granted to any
+# person obtaining a copy of this software, associated documentation and/or data
+# (collectively the "Software"), free of charge and under any and all copyright
+# rights in the Software, and any and all patent rights owned or freely
+# licensable by each licensor hereunder covering either (i) the unmodified
+# Software as contributed to or provided by such licensor, or (ii) the Larger
+# Works (as defined below), to deal in both
+#
+# (a) the Software, and
+#
+# (b) any piece of software and/or hardware listed in the lrgrwrks.txt file if
+# one is included with the Software (each a "Larger Work" to which the Software
+# is contributed by such licensors),
+#
+# without restriction, including without limitation the rights to copy, create
+# derivative works of, display, perform, and distribute the Software and make,
+# use, sell, offer for sale, import, export, have made, and have sold the
+# Software and the Larger Work(s), and to sublicense the foregoing rights on
+# either these or other terms.
+#
+# This license is subject to the following condition:
+#
+# The above copyright notice and either this complete permission notice or at
+# a minimum a reference to the UPL must be included in all copies or
+# substantial portions of the Software.
+#
+# THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR
+# IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY,
+# FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
+# AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER
+# LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM,
+# OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE
+# SOFTWARE.
+#
+
+#
+# Generate sample PKI for TLS testing
+#
+# The certificate hierarchy is as follows:
+#
+#   root -+-> clientca ---> client
+#         |
+#         +-> serverca -+-> rsa_server
+#                       |
+#                       +-> ec_server
+#                       |
+#                       +-> dsa_server
+#
+# The clientca certificate is trusted by the server for client authentication.
+# The serverca certificate is trusted by the client.
+#
+# The main products of this script are server.<ext>, client.<ext>,
+# clienttrust.<ext> and servertrust.<ext>, where <ext> is jks or p12.
+#
+# The client.<ext> keystore contains the client end-entity certificate and
+# private key along with the clientca and root certificates.
+#
+# The server.<ext> keystore contains the three server end-entity certificates
+# (rsa_server, ec_server and dsa_server) and their corresponding private keys
+# along with the serverca and root certificates.
+#
+# The clienttrust.<ext> keystore contains the serverca certificate while the
+# servertrust.<ext> keystore contains the clientca certificate.
+#
+
+# Deterine how to run keytool (and java & javac)
+if [ -n "$JAVA_HOME" ] ; then
+    KEYTOOL_CMD="$JAVA_HOME/bin/keytool"
+    JAVA_CMD="$JAVA_HOME/bin/java"
+    JAVAC_CMD="$JAVA_HOME/bin/javac"
+else
+    KEYTOOL_CMD=keytool
+    JAVA_CMD=java
+    JAVAC_CMD=javac
+fi
+if ! command -v  "$KEYTOOL_CMD" >/dev/null 2>&1 ; then
+   echo "Failed to run java keytool." >&2
+   echo "   Check your PATH or JAVA_HOME environment variable settings facilitate running java keytool" >&2
+   exit 1
+fi
+
+# Check that the Java version supports RFC 9879. At the time of writing this was JDK 26+
+KEYTOOL_JAVA_MAJOR_VERSION=$($KEYTOOL_CMD -J-version 2>&1 | awk -F[\".] '/version/ {print $2}')
+echo "Current keytool java runtime major version: $KEYTOOL_JAVA_MAJOR_VERSION."
+if [ $KEYTOOL_JAVA_MAJOR_VERSION -lt '26' ]; then
+    echo "The keytool java runtime version must support RFC 9879."
+    echo "Please rerun this script with PATH or JAVA_HOME set to use a java keytool with java runtime version 26 or later."
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+mkdir -p build/generated/pki
+cd build/generated/pki
+
+# Configure keytool to create FIPS compliant pkcs12 keystores - see RFC 9879
+KEYTOOL_CMD+=" -J-Dkeystore.pkcs12.keyProtectionAlgorithm=PBEWithHmacSHA256andAES_256"
+KEYTOOL_CMD+=" -J-Dkeystore.pkcs12.certProtectionAlgorithm=PBEWithHmacSHA256andAES_256"
+KEYTOOL_CMD+=" -J-Dkeystore.pkcs12.macAlgorithm=PBEWithHmacSHA256"
+
+serverip=127.0.0.1
+pass=Password1
+validity=`expr 365 \* 5` # 5 years
+
+# Set the root and intermediate CA certificate key algorithms
+# RSA, DSA or EC
+root_keyalg=RSA
+ca_keyalg=RSA
+
+# Set the client end-entity key algorithm and Key Usage
+client_ee_keyalg=RSA
+client_eeku=digitalSignature
+
+# Set the store type
+#ext=jks
+#st=jks
+ext=p12
+st=pkcs12
+
+rm -f root.pem serverca.pem clientca.pem client.pem
+rm -f rsa_server.pem ec_server.pem dsa_server.pem
+rm -f root.$ext serverca.$ext server.$ext clientca.$ext client.$ext
+rm -f clienttrust.$ext servertrust.$ext genpki_log.txt
+
+echo "Certificate validity: $validity days"
+
+#
+# Get the default key generation parameters to use for the given key type.
+# Sets the keyparam shell variable.
+#
+# Usage: get_keyparam <1:keyalg>
+#
+get_keyparam()
+{
+    case $1 in
+    rsa|RSA|dsa|DSA)
+        keyparam="-keysize 2048" ;;
+    ec|EC)
+        keyparam="-keysize 256" ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+#
+# Get the default signature algorithm to use for the given key type.
+# Sets the sigalg shell variable.
+#
+# Usage: get_sigalg <1:keyalg>
+#
+get_sigalg()
+{
+    case $1 in
+    rsa|RSA)
+        sigalg="SHA256withRSA" ;;
+    dsa|DSA)
+        sigalg="SHA256withDSA" ;;
+    ec|EC)
+        sigalg="SHA256withECDSA" ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+#
+# Generate a key-pair.
+#
+# Usage: genkeypair <1:alias> <2:keyalg> <3:certext> <4:dname> <5:keystore>
+#
+genkeypair()
+{
+    echo "Generating $2 key-pair for $1"
+    get_keyparam $2
+    get_sigalg $2
+    $KEYTOOL_CMD -keypass $pass -storepass $pass -genkeypair -keyalg $2 $keyparam \
+        -sigalg $sigalg -storetype $st -keystore "$5" -alias $1 $3                \
+        -validity $validity -dname "$4" 2>> genpki_log.txt
+}
+
+#
+# Import a certificate into the specified keystore.
+#
+# Usage: importcert <1:alias> <2:pem> <3:keystore>
+#
+importcert()
+{
+    echo "Importing $2 into $3 as $1"
+    $KEYTOOL_CMD -storepass $pass -storetype $st -keystore "$3" -importcert       \
+        -alias $1 -file "$2" -noprompt 2>> genpki_log.txt
+}
+
+#
+# Generate a certificate for the specified key-pair and replace the self-signed
+# placeholder certificate with the generated certificate.
+#
+# Usage: gencert <1:alias> <2:keyalg> <3:signer_alias> <4:signer_keyalg>
+#                <5:certext> <6:certs> <7:keystore>
+#
+gencert()
+{
+    get_sigalg $4
+    signer_sigalg=$sigalg
+    get_sigalg $2
+    echo "Issuing certificate for $1 signed by $3 using $signer_sigalg"
+    $KEYTOOL_CMD -keypass $pass -storepass $pass -storetype $st -keystore "$7"    \
+        -certreq -sigalg $sigalg -alias $1 |
+            $KEYTOOL_CMD $SECURITY_PROPERTIES_CLAUSE                              \
+                -keypass $pass -storepass $pass -storetype $st                    \
+                -keystore "$3.$ext" -gencert -sigalg $signer_sigalg               \
+                -validity $validity -alias $3 $5 -rfc > $1.pem
+
+# Import the issued certificate into the keystore, replacing the self-signed
+# placeholder certificate, along with the rest of the certificate chain
+    echo "Importing certs ($6 $1.pem) into $7"
+    cat $6 $1.pem |
+        $KEYTOOL_CMD -storepass $pass -storetype $st -keystore "$7"               \
+            -importcert -alias $1 -noprompt 2>> genpki_log.txt
+}
+
+#
+# Generate a key-pair and issue a new certificate signed by the specified
+# signer.
+#
+# Usage: issuecert <1:alias> <2:keyalg> <3:signer_alias> <4:signer_keyalg>
+#                  <5:genkey_certext> <6:dname> <7:gencert_certext> <8:certs>
+#                  <9:keystore>
+#
+issuecert()
+{
+    genkeypair $1 $2 "$5" "$6" $9
+    gencert $1 $2 $3 $4 "$7" "$8" $9
+}
+
+# Generate key-pair for root certificate
+echo "Generating root key-pair"
+genkeypair root $root_keyalg "-ext bc:c" "OU=Root Certificate Authority, O=BigCA, L=Cupertino, ST=CA, C=US" root.$ext
+
+# Export the self-signed root certificate
+echo "Exporting root cert"
+$KEYTOOL_CMD -storepass $pass -storetype $st -keystore root.$ext -alias root      \
+    -exportcert -rfc > root.pem
+
+echo "*** Issuing certificates"
+
+# Issue intermediate CA certificate (server-CA)
+issuecert serverca $ca_keyalg root $root_keyalg "-ext bc:c"                       \
+    "OU=Certificate Authority 1, O=BigCA, L=Cupertino, ST=CA, C=US"               \
+    "-ext BC=0" root.pem serverca.$ext
+
+# Issue RSA end entity certificate (server-EE)
+eeku=digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
+issuecert rsa_server RSA serverca $ca_keyalg ""                                   \
+    "CN=$serverip, OU=Widget Development Group, O=Ficticious Widgets\\, Inc., L=San Mateo, ST=CA, C=US" \
+    "-ext KU:c=$eeku -ext SAN=IP:$serverip" "root.pem serverca.pem" server.$ext
+
+# Issue EC end entity certificate (server-EE)
+eeku=digitalSignature,keyAgreement
+issuecert ec_server EC serverca $ca_keyalg ""                                     \
+    "CN=$serverip, OU=Widget Development Group, O=Ficticious Widgets\\, Inc., L=San Mateo, ST=CA, C=US" \
+    "-ext KU:c=$eeku -ext SAN=IP:$serverip" "root.pem serverca.pem" server.$ext
+
+# Issue DSA end entity certificate (server-EE)
+eeku=digitalSignature
+issuecert dsa_server DSA serverca $ca_keyalg ""                                   \
+    "CN=$serverip, OU=Widget Development Group, O=Ficticious Widgets\\, Inc., L=San Mateo, ST=CA, C=US" \
+    "-ext KU:c=$eeku -ext SAN=IP:$serverip" "root.pem serverca.pem" server.$ext
+
+# Issue intermediate CA certificate (client-CA)
+issuecert clientca $ca_keyalg root $root_keyalg "-ext bc:c"                       \
+    "OU=Certificate Authority 2, O=BigCA, L=Cupertino, ST=CA, C=US"               \
+    "-ext BC=0" root.pem clientca.$ext
+
+# Issue end entity certificate for client authentication (client-EE)
+issuecert client $client_ee_keyalg clientca $ca_keyalg ""                         \
+    "CN=Duke, OU=Java Software, O=Oracle, L=Cupertino, ST=CA, C=US"               \
+    "-ext KU:c=$client_eeku" "root.pem clientca.pem" client.$ext
+
+echo "*** Creating trust stores"
+
+# Create a trust store for the server that contains the client CA certificate
+importcert clientca clientca.pem servertrust.$ext
+# Create a trust store for the client that contains the server CA certificate
+importcert serverca serverca.pem clienttrust.$ext
+
+# Workaround for the absence of PKCS#12 KeyStore support for RFC 9879 in JDK versions less than 26 - See JDK-8343232
+# This can be removed when support for RFC 9879 is backported to JDK 25
+echo "*** Creating a copy of each keystore with keystore.pkcs12.macAlgorithm set to NONE"
+
+# Create a java utility that sets a PKCS12 keystore mac algorithm to NONE
+cat > MacAlgNoneUtil.java <<MAC_ALG_NON_UTIL_DELIMITER
+import java.io.FileInputStream;
+import java.io.FileOutputStream;
+import java.io.InputStream;
+import java.io.OutputStream;
+
+import java.security.KeyStore;
+import java.security.Security;
+
+import java.util.Enumeration;
+
+public final class MacAlgNoneUtil {
+    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {
+        if (args.length < 3) {
+            System.err.println("Usage: MacAlgUtil <input keystore> <output keystore>, <password>");
+            System.exit(1);
+        }
+        String inFilename = args[0];
+        String outFilename = args[1];
+        char[] password = args[2].toCharArray();
+
+        KeyStore.ProtectionParameter protectionParameter = new KeyStore.PasswordProtection(password);
+
+        Security.setProperty("keystore.pkcs12.macAlgorithm", "NONE");
+
+        KeyStore inKeyStore  = KeyStore.getInstance("PKCS12");
+        KeyStore outKeyStore = KeyStore.getInstance("PKCS12");
+
+        InputStream in = new FileInputStream(inFilename);
+        inKeyStore.load(in, password);
+        in.close();
+
+        outKeyStore.load(null, null);
+
+        Enumeration<String> es = inKeyStore.aliases();
+        while (es.hasMoreElements()) {
+            String alias = es.nextElement();
+            try {
+                KeyStore.Entry entry = inKeyStore.getEntry(alias, protectionParameter);
+                outKeyStore.setEntry(alias, entry, protectionParameter);
+            } catch (java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException e) {
+                // Trusted certificate entries are not password-protected
+                KeyStore.Entry entry = inKeyStore.getEntry(alias, null);
+                outKeyStore.setEntry(alias, entry, null);
+            }
+        }
+
+        OutputStream out = new FileOutputStream(outFilename);
+        outKeyStore.store(out, password);
+        out.close();
+    }
+}
+MAC_ALG_NON_UTIL_DELIMITER
+
+# Compile the java utility that sets a PKCS12 keystore mac algorithm to NONE
+"$JAVAC_CMD" MacAlgNoneUtil.java
+
+mkdir -p nomac
+
+# Creating a copy of each keystore with keystore.pkcs12.macAlgorithm set to NONE
+for keystore in *.p12; do
+   echo ${keystore}
+   "$JAVA_CMD" MacAlgNoneUtil ${keystore} nomac/${keystore} $pass
+done
+
+rm -f MacAlgNoneUtil.java MacAlgNoneUtil.class

+ * (b) any piece of software and/or hardware listed in the lrgrwrks.txt file if

+package com.oracle.test.integration.keyfactory;
+
+import java.security.KeyFactory;
+import java.security.spec.PKCS8EncodedKeySpec;
+import java.util.Arrays;
+
+import org.junit.Test;
+
+import com.oracle.jiphertest.util.ProviderUtil;
+import com.oracle.jiphertest.util.TestUtil;
+
+public class ECKeyFactoryTest {
+
+    KeyFactory kf;
+
+    public ECKeyFactoryTest() throws Exception {
+        kf = ProviderUtil.getKeyFactory("EC");
+    }
+
+    @Test
+    public void rfc5915NonCompliantPrivateKeyEncoding() throws Exception {
+        // The following EC private key PKCS #8 ASN.1 encodings do NOT comply with RFC 5915 section 3:
+        //    The private key is an octet string of length ceiling (log2(n)/8) (where n is the order of the curve)
+        //    obtained from the unsigned integer via the Integer-to-Octet-String-Primitive (I2OSP)
+        byte[] secp224r1 = TestUtil.hexToBytes("3039020100301006072A8648CE3D020106052B8104002104223020020101041B75E29141B82FEFCEE01A1BF3453BD37BE463F65B4AF84976B74E08");
+        byte[] secp256r1 = TestUtil.hexToBytes("3040020100301306072A8648CE3D020106082A8648CE3D03010704263024020101041F4D718710022B198A4FB5EEC93445DE85C2B65DB2E5CB29CAB24F97E00EA431");
+        byte[] secp384r1 = TestUtil.hexToBytes("304D020100301006072A8648CE3D020106052B8104002204363034020101042FDF18F4FBA2D72C2954975A91DD5C0483C2E44CCFF3C184741FE790168A3A20BBF8DD23E46555D2D05B0BC99528E9C5");
+        byte[] secp521r1 = TestUtil.hexToBytes("305F020100301006072A8648CE3D020106052B810400230448304602010104416D0E393A538376860B377C92DB7A3A410B20DF7F5FD729EFC4703B7AD55F329C6E78EF446E03FE62038E2661E2D4BD01C8BA27F3D940E389E8700A57F014AD7AF3");
+        for (byte[] encoding : Arrays.asList(secp224r1, secp256r1, secp384r1, secp521r1)) {
+            kf.generatePrivate(new PKCS8EncodedKeySpec(encoding));
+        }
+    }
+}

+public class SymCipherGetInstanceTest {

+package com.oracle.test.integration.tls;
+
+public enum HandshakeMessageType {
+    CLIENT_HELLO((byte)0x01, "client_hello"),
+    SERVER_HELLO((byte)0x02, "server_hello"),
+    NEW_SESSION_TICKET((byte)0x04, "new_session_ticket"),
+    END_OF_EARLY_DATA((byte)0x05, "end_of_early_data"),
+    CERTIFICATE((byte)0x0B, "certificate"),
+    SERVER_KEY_EXCHANGE((byte)0x0C, "server_key_exchange"),
+    CERTIFICATE_REQUEST((byte)0x0D, "certificate_request"),
+    SERVER_HELLO_DONE((byte)0x0E, "server_hello_done"),
+    CERTIFICATE_VERIFY((byte)0x0F, "certificate_verify"),
+    CLIENT_KEY_EXCHANGE((byte)0x10, "client_key_exchange"),
+    FINISHED((byte)0x14, "finished"),
+    CERTIFICATE_STATUS((byte)0x16, "certificate_status"),
+    KEY_UPDATE((byte)0x18, "key_update");
+
+    final byte value;
+    final String name;
+
+    HandshakeMessageType(byte value, String name) {
+        this.value = value;
+        this.name = name;
+    }
+
+    public static HandshakeMessageType byValue(byte value) {
+        for (HandshakeMessageType messageType : HandshakeMessageType.values()) {
+            if (messageType.value == value) {
+                return messageType;
+            }
+        }
+        return null;
+    }
+}

+package com.oracle.jiphertest.helpers;
+
+import java.security.spec.ECFieldFp;
+import java.security.spec.ECParameterSpec;
+import java.security.spec.ECPoint;
+import java.security.spec.EllipticCurve;
+
+import static com.oracle.jiphertest.util.TestUtil.hexToBigInt;
+
+public class EcParamSpecHelper {
+
+    public static ECParameterSpec createSpec(CurveData data) {
+        return createSpec(data.primeHex, data.aHex, data.bHex, data.gxHex, data.gyHex, data.nHex, data.cofactor);
+    }
+
+    public static ECParameterSpec createSpec(String primeHex, String coeffAHex, String coeffBHex, String gxhex, String gyHex, String orderHex, int cofactor) {
+        return new ECParameterSpec(new EllipticCurve(new ECFieldFp(hexToBigInt(primeHex)), hexToBigInt(coeffAHex), hexToBigInt(coeffBHex)),
+                new ECPoint(hexToBigInt(gxhex), hexToBigInt(gyHex)),
+                hexToBigInt(orderHex), cofactor);
+    }
+}

+package com.oracle.jiphertest.helpers;
+
+import java.security.Provider;
+import java.security.Security;
+import java.util.List;
+
+public class ProviderSetup {
+    static public void limitProviders(List<String> requiredProviderNames) {
+        // Dynamically unregister all cryptographic service providers other that those required.
+        for (Provider provider : Security.getProviders()) {
+            String providerName = provider.getName();
+            if (!requiredProviderNames.contains(providerName)) {
+                Security.removeProvider(providerName);
+            }
+        }
+    }
+}

+/**

+public class TestAssert {
+
+    public static void assertArrayEquals(String msg, byte[] one, int off1, byte[] two, int off2, int len) {
+        Assert.assertArrayEquals(msg, Arrays.copyOfRange(one, off1, off1+len), Arrays.copyOfRange(two, off2, off2+len));
+    }
+
+    public static void assertArrayEquals(byte[] one, int off1, byte[] two, int off2, int len) {
+        Assert.assertArrayEquals(Arrays.copyOfRange(one, off1, off1+len), Arrays.copyOfRange(two, off2, off2+len));
+    }
+
+}

+package com.oracle.jiphertest.util;

+import java.time.LocalDateTime;
+import java.time.ZoneOffset;
+import java.time.temporal.TemporalAccessor;
+
+public class TimeUtil {
+
+    public static TemporalAccessor dt(LocalDateTime ldt, ZoneOffset tz, int newMinute, int newSecond, int newNano) {
+        if (newMinute != -1) {
+            ldt = ldt.withMinute(newMinute);
+        }
+        if (newSecond != -1) {
+            ldt = ldt.withSecond(newSecond);
+        }
+        if (newNano != -1) {
+            ldt = ldt.withNano(newNano);
+        }
+        return tz != null ? ldt.atOffset(tz) : ldt;
+    }
+
+}

+package com.oracle.jiphertest.util;
+
+import java.io.InputStream;
+import java.lang.reflect.Method;
+
+public class X509FactoryUtil {
+    public static void clearCertCache() {
+        try {
+            Class<?> cls = Class.forName("sun.security.provider.X509Factory");
+            Object x509Factory = cls.getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance();
+            Method m = cls.getMethod("engineGenerateCertificate", InputStream.class);
+            m.invoke(x509Factory, (InputStream) null);
+        } catch (Exception ignored) {}
+    }
+}

+module com.oracle.jiphertest.other {
+    exports com.oracle.jiphertest.helpers;
+    exports com.oracle.jiphertest.model;
+    exports com.oracle.jiphertest.testdata;
+    exports com.oracle.jiphertest.util;
+
+    // The com.oracle.jiphertest.util.ProviderUtil class creates an instance of com.oracle.jipher.provider.JipherJCE to
+    // dynamically register it as a java security provider.
+    // The com.oracle.jiphertest.util.FipsProviderInfoUtil class creates an instance of
+    // com.oracle.jipher.provider.JipherJCE from which it can query information about the OpenSSL FIPS provider version.
+    // The com.oracle.systest.classloader.CipherTest system test uses com.oracle.jiphertest.testdata to load test data,
+    // but it does NOT list jipher-jce on the classpath because it uses a URLClassLoader to load the jipher-jce JAR.
+    // Consequently, the following 'requires' statement is 'static' to indicate that the dependency is required
+    // at compile time but is optional at run time.
+    requires static com.oracle.jipher;
+
+    // testdata uses gson
+    requires com.google.gson;
+    // Allow com.google.gson to use reflection on the classes in this module's classes. Prevents:
+    //      "module com.oracle.jiphertest.other does not opens com.oracle.jiphertest.testdata" to module com.google.gson
+    opens com.oracle.jiphertest.testdata to com.google.gson;
+
+    // com.oracle.jiphertest.helpers.TlsServer used org.junit.Assert
+    requires junit; // Automatic module from junit:junit
+}

+# (collectively the "Software"), free of charge and under any and all copyright
+# rights in the Software, and any and all patent rights owned or freely
+# licensable by each licensor hereunder covering either (i) the unmodified
+# Software as contributed to or provided by such licensor, or (ii) the Larger
+# Works (as defined below), to deal in both
+#

+#
+# (b) any piece of software and/or hardware listed in the lrgrwrks.txt file if
+# one is included with the Software (each a "Larger Work" to which the Software
+# is contributed by such licensors),

+# derivative works of, display, perform, and distribute the Software and make,
+# use, sell, offer for sale, import, export, have made, and have sold the
+# Software and the Larger Work(s), and to sublicense the foregoing rights on
+# either these or other terms.

+# a minimum a reference to the UPL must be included in all copies or
+# substantial portions of the Software.

+# IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY,
+# FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
+# AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER
+# LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM,
+# OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE
+# SOFTWARE.
+#
+
+# Generates PBMAC1 PKCS12 keystores using different providers and MAC algorithms.
+# Requires:
+#  JDK version 26
+#  OpenSSL Version 3.4 or later
+
+function print_usage()
+{
+   echo "$0 (--keystore-provider <SUN|OpenSSL>) (--mac-algorithm <hmacsha1|hmacsha224|hmacsha256|hmacsha384|hmacsha512>) (--help)"
+}
+
+function get_java_major_version()
+{
+   java_version=$(java -version 2>&1)
+   echo "$java_version" | grep version | grep -oE '[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)*' | head -n 1 | awk -F. '{print ($1 == "1") ? $2 : $1}'
+}
+
+function get_openssl_minor_version()
+{
+   openssl_version=$(openssl -version | awk '{print $2}')
+   echo "$openssl_version" | awk -F. '{print $2}'
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+   case $1 in
+    --help)
+      print_usage
+      exit 1
+      ;;
+    --keystore-provider)
+      KEYSTORE_PROVIDER="$2"
+      shift # past argument
+      shift # past value
+      ;;
+    --mac-algorithm)
+      MAC_ALGORITHM="$2"
+      shift # past argument
+      shift # past value
+      ;;
+       *)
+      >&2 echo "Unsupported option: $1"
+      >&2 print_usage
+      exit 1
+      ;;
+   esac
+done
+
+
+if [ -z ${KEYSTORE_PROVIDER+x} ]; then
+  KEYSTORE_PROVIDER="SUN"
+fi
+
+case $KEYSTORE_PROVIDER in
+  SUN)
+    MAJOR_VERSION=$(get_java_major_version)
+    if [[ ${MAJOR_VERSION} -lt 26 ]]; then
+      >&2 echo "Unsupported Java version: ${MAJOR_VERSION}. Minimum supported Java version: 26"
+      exit 1
+    fi
+    ;;
+  OpenSSL)
+    if [[ $(get_openssl_minor_version) -lt 4 ]]; then
+      >&2 echo "Unsupported OpenSSL version $(openssl -version | awk '{print $2}'). Minimum supported OpenSSL version: 3.4.0"
+      exit -1
+    fi
+    ;;
+  *)
+    >&2 echo "Unsupported keystore provider: $KEYSTORE_PROVIDER"
+    >&2 print_usage
+    exit 1
+    ;;
+esac
+
+if [ -z ${MAC_ALGORITHM+x} ]; then
+  MAC_ALGORITHM="hmacsha256"
+fi
+
+case $MAC_ALGORITHM in
+  hmacsha1)
+    MD="sha1"
+    ;;
+  hmacsha224)
+    MD="sha224"
+    ;;
+  hmacsha256)
+    MD="sha256"
+    ;;
+ hmacsha384)
+    MD="sha384"
+    ;;
+  hmacsha512)
+    MD="sha512"
+    ;;
+  *)
+    >&2 echo "Unsupported mac algorithm: $MAC_ALGORTHM"
+    >&2 print_usage
+    exit 1
+    ;;
+esac
+
+if [ "${KEYSTORE_PROVIDER}" == "OpenSSL" ]; then
+  PBE="AES-256-CBC"
+else
+  PBE="PBEWithHmacSHA512andAES_256"
+fi
+
+PASSWORD="password"
+ALIAS=test
+
+if [ "${KEYSTORE_PROVIDER}" == "OpenSSL" ]; then
+  SUBJECT="/C=AU/ST=QLD/L=Brisbane/O=Oracle/OU=OCI/CN=Test"
+
+  PBE_CLAUSE="-certpbe ${PBE} -keypbe ${PBE}"
+  PBMAC1_CLAUSE="-pbmac1_pbkdf2 -pbmac1_pbkdf2_md ${MD} -macsaltlen 16"
+  FILENAME="OpenSSL.pbmac1.${MAC_ALGORITHM}.keystore.p12"
+
+  rm -f ${ALIAS}.private.key.pem ${ALIAS}.cert.pem ${FILENAME}
+
+  # Create test private key and self signed ca
+  openssl req -newkey rsa:2048 -sha256 -nodes -keyout ${ALIAS}.private.key.pem -x509 -days 7300 -out ${ALIAS}.cert.pem -subj "${SUBJECT}"
+
+  # Wrap up content in pkcs12
+  openssl pkcs12 -export -in ${ALIAS}.cert.pem -inkey ${ALIAS}.private.key.pem \
+                 -name ${ALIAS} -out ${FILENAME} -passout pass:${PASSWORD} \
+                 ${PBE_CLAUSE} ${PBMAC1_CLAUSE}
+
+else
+  DNAME="CN=Test, OU=OCI, O=Oracle, L=Brisbane, ST=QLD, C=AU"
+
+  SECURITY_PROPERTIES_CLAUSE=" -J-Dkeystore.pkcs12.keyProtectionAlgorithm=${PBE} "
+  SECURITY_PROPERTIES_CLAUSE+="-J-Dkeystore.pkcs12.certProtectionAlgorithm=${PBE} "
+  SECURITY_PROPERTIES_CLAUSE+="-J-Dkeystore.pkcs12.macAlgorithm=pbewith${MAC_ALGORITHM}"
+
+  FILENAME="${KEYSTORE_PROVIDER}.pbmac1.${MAC_ALGORITHM}.keystore.p12"
+  rm -f ${FILENAME}
+
+  keytool -keystore ${FILENAME} -genkey -alias ${ALIAS} -keyalg "rsa" -dname "${DNAME}" \
+          -destprovidername ${KEYSTORE_PROVIDER} -deststoretype pkcs12 \
+          -deststorepass ${PASSWORD} -destkeypass ${PASSWORD} \
+          ${SECURITY_PROPERTIES_CLAUSE}
+fi

+package com.oracle.jiphertest.leak.util;
+
+import java.lang.reflect.Method;
+
+import com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl.OpenSsl;
+import com.oracle.jipher.provider.JipherJCE;
+
+public class OpenSslFactoryUtil {
+    private static final String OPENSSL_FACTORY_CLASS_NAME = "com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl.OpenSslFactory";
+
+    public static OpenSsl getOpenSslSingleton() {
+
+        // Trigger OpenSslFactory static initialisation
+        // OpenSslFactory static initialisation must happen before the class is accessed via reflection
+        new JipherJCE().getInfo();
+
+        try {
+            Class<?> cls = Class.forName(OPENSSL_FACTORY_CLASS_NAME);
+            Method getInstance = cls.getDeclaredMethod("getInstance");
+            getInstance.setAccessible(true);
+            return (OpenSsl) getInstance.invoke(null);
+        } catch (ReflectiveOperationException ex) {
+            throw new Error(ex);
+        }
+    }
+}

+package com.oracle.jiphertest.leak.util;
+
+import java.util.List;
+
+public class PreFetchUtil {
+    static final private String PACKAGE_NAME = "com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl";
+    static final private List<String> CLASS_NAMES  = List.of("CipherCtx", "Kdf", "MacCtx", "MdCtx");
+
+    public static void triggerPrefetch() {
+        try {
+            for (String className: CLASS_NAMES) {
+                Class<?> cls = Class.forName(PACKAGE_NAME + "." + className);
+            }
+        } catch (ReflectiveOperationException ex) {
+            throw new Error(ex);
+        }
+    }
+}

+    UniversalString(28),
+    BMPString(30),
+
+    // "useful types"
+    UTCTime(23),
+    GeneralizedTime(24);

+    public final int tagValue;

Note: The OpenSSL FIPS provider still enforces FIPS restrictions + */ +public class Fips { + + /** + * An enum representing FIPS enforcement policies + */ + public enum EnforcementPolicy { + FIPS, // Permits 'Acceptable' and 'Legacy use' as defined in SP 800-131A Rev. 2. + FIPS_STRICT, // Permits 'Acceptable' use ONLY as defined in SP 800-131A Rev. 2. Does not allow 'Legacy use'. + NONE, // No enforcement is applied by the bytecode. + // Note: The OpenSSL FIPS provider still enforces FIPS restrictions. + } + + private Fips() { + // Prevent instantiation + } + + static final Debug DEBUG = Debug.getInstance("jipher"); + + + private static final Policy ENFORCER = initPolicy(); + + /** + * Get the toolkit enforcement policy. + * @return the policy configured for the toolkit + */ + public static Policy enforcement() { + return ENFORCER; + } + + private static Policy initPolicy() { + Policy policy = switch (ToolkitProperties.getFipsEnforcementValue()) { + case NONE -> none(); + case FIPS_STRICT -> fipsStrictPolicy(); + default -> fipsPolicy(); + }; + DEBUG.println("Setting FIPS enforcement policy = " + policy.getPolicyName()); + return policy; + } + + /** + * Create the FIPS_STRICT policy. This policy defines rules according to the 'Acceptable' and 'Legacy use' guidelines + * as defined in SP 800-131A Rev. 2. This is the default. + * + * @return the created Policy + */ + static Policy fipsPolicy() { + return new Policy.Builder(EnforcementPolicy.FIPS.name()) + .keyTypeRule((keyType) -> !((String) keyType).equalsIgnoreCase("DSA"), SIGN) // FIPS 140-3 IG, section C.K Legacy Use + .strengthRule("RSA", (keyBits) -> (int) keyBits >= 2048, ENCRYPT_ASYM, DECRYPT_ASYM, KEYGEN, SIGN) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 2, Acceptable + .strengthRule("RSA", (keyBits) -> (int) keyBits >= 1024, VERIFY) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 2, Legacy use + .strengthRule("EC", (curveBits) -> (int) curveBits >= 224, KEYGEN, SIGN, KEYAGREE) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 2, Acceptable + .strengthRule("EC", (curveBits) -> (int) curveBits >= 160, VERIFY) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 2, Legacy use + .strengthRule("DSA", (paramBits) -> ((int[]) paramBits)[0] >= 512, VERIFY) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 2, Legacy use + .strengthRule("DH", (keyBits) -> (int) keyBits >= 2048, KEYAGREE, KEYGEN) + .strengthRule("HMAC", (keyBits) -> (int) keyBits >= 0, MAC) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 9 HMAC Verification, Legacy use + .strengthRule("KDF", (keyBits) -> (int) keyBits >= 112, KEYGEN, KEYDERIVE) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Section 8 + .strengthRule("AES", (keyBits) -> (int) keyBits >= 128, ENCRYPT_SYM, DECRYPT_SYM) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 1, Acceptable + .strengthRule("DESede", (keyBits) -> (int) keyBits == 192, ENCRYPT_SYM, DECRYPT_SYM) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 1, Legacy use + .algRule((mdAlg) -> !MdAlg.SHA1.getAlg().equals(mdAlg), SIGN) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 8, Legacy use + .algRule((cipherAlg) -> !((String) cipherAlg).contains("DESede"), ENCRYPT_SYM) // SP 800-131A Rev. 2 Table 1, Legacy use + .build(); + } + + /** + * Create a policy which enforces nothing. + * + * @return the created Policy + */ + static Policy none() { + return new Policy.Builder(EnforcementPolicy.NONE.name()).build(); + } + +} + +

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.key;
+
+import java.math.BigInteger;
+import java.security.interfaces.DSAPublicKey;
+import java.security.spec.DSAParameterSpec;
+
+import com.oracle.jipher.internal.asn1.Asn1BerValue;
+import com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl.Pkey;
+
+import static com.oracle.jipher.internal.asn1.Asn1.newBitString;
+import static com.oracle.jipher.internal.asn1.Asn1.newInteger;
+import static com.oracle.jipher.internal.asn1.Asn1.newOid;
+import static com.oracle.jipher.internal.asn1.Asn1.newSequence;
+
+/**
+ * {@link DSAPublicKey} implementation with an {@code EVP_PKEY} backed key.
+ */
+public final class JceDsaPublicKey extends JceOsslPublicKey implements DSAPublicKey {
+
+    private static final String ALG = "DSA";
+
+    private static final Asn1BerValue ID_DSA = newOid("1.2.840.10040.4.1");
+
+    private final BigInteger pub;
+    private final DSAParameterSpec params;
+
+    public JceDsaPublicKey(Pkey pkey, BigInteger pub, DSAParameterSpec params, byte[] encoding) {
+        super(ALG, pkey, encoding);
+        this.pub = pub;
+        this.params = params;
+    }
+
+    @Override
+    public BigInteger getY() {
+        return this.pub;
+    }
+
+    @Override
+    public DSAParameterSpec getParams() {
+        return this.params;
+    }

+     * Encode the DSA public key into X.509 SubjectPublicKeyInfo DER format.
+     *
+     * @return the DER-encoded X.509 SubjectPublicKeyInfo of the DSA public
+     * key.
+     */
+    @Override
+    byte[] derEncode() {
+        // SubjectPublicKeyInfo
+        Asn1BerValue dsaPubKey = newSequence(
+                // algorithm AlgorithmIdentifier
+                newSequence(
+                        // algorithm OBJECT IDENTIFIER
+                        ID_DSA,
+                        // parameters ANY OPTIONAL
+                        newSequence(
+                                newInteger(this.params.getP()),
+                                newInteger(this.params.getQ()),
+                                newInteger(this.params.getG())
+                        )
+                ),
+
+                // subjectPublicKey BIT STRING
+                newBitString(
+                        // DSAPublicKey ::= INTEGER -- public key, Y
+                        newInteger(this.pub).encodeDerOctets()
+                )
+        );
+        return dsaPubKey.encodeDerOctets();
+    }
+
+    @Override
+    public String toString() {
+        return "DsaPublicKey: params=[P=" + this.params.getP() +
+                ",Q=" + this.params.getQ() +
+                ",G=" + this.params.getG() +
+                "],pubValue=" + this.pub;
+    }
+}

+        derive(key, OpenSsl.getInstance().dataParamBuffer(params));

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl;
+
+public interface EVP_MD extends OsslEvpAlgorithm {
+
+    /* The following are OpenSSL API md constants defined in evp.h */
+
+    int MAX_MD_SIZE = 64;
+
+    /* The following are OpenSSL API md constants defined in core_names.h */
+
+    /* Known DIGEST names (not a complete list) */
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA1            = "SHA1";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA2_224        = "SHA2-224";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA2_256        = "SHA2-256";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA2_384        = "SHA2-384";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA2_512        = "SHA2-512";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA2_512_224    = "SHA2-512/224";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA2_512_256    = "SHA2-512/256";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA3_224        = "SHA3-224";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA3_256        = "SHA3-256";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA3_384        = "SHA3-384";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_SHA3_512        = "SHA3-512";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_KECCAK_KMAC128  = "KECCAK-KMAC-128";
+    String DIGEST_NAME_KECCAK_KMAC256  = "KECCAK-KMAC-256";
+
+    /* digest parameters */
+    String DIGEST_PARAM_XOFLEN         = "xoflen";        /* size_t */
+    String DIGEST_PARAM_SSL3_MS        = "ssl3-ms";       /* octet_string */
+    String DIGEST_PARAM_PAD_TYPE       = "pad-type";      /* uint */
+    String DIGEST_PARAM_MICALG         = "micalg";        /* utf8_string */
+    String DIGEST_PARAM_BLOCK_SIZE     = "blocksize";     /* size_t */
+    String DIGEST_PARAM_SIZE           = "size";          /* size_t */
+    String DIGEST_PARAM_XOF            = "xof";           /* int, 0 or 1 */
+    String DIGEST_PARAM_ALGID_ABSENT   = "algid-absent";  /* int, 0 or 1 */
+
+    default EVP_MD upRef() {
+        return upRef(OsslArena.ofAuto());
+    }
+    EVP_MD upRef(OsslArena arena);
+
+    int blockSize();
+    int size();
+}

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl;
+
+public interface EVP_RAND_CTX extends OsslSetParams {
+
+    /* OpenSSL API random context state constants defined in evp.h */
+    enum State {
+        UNINITIALISED, READY, ERROR
+    }
+
+    void instantiate(int strength, boolean predictionResistance, byte[] pStr, OsslParamBuffer paramBuffer);
+    default void instantiate(int strength, boolean predictionResistance, byte[] pStr, OSSL_PARAM... params) {
+        instantiate(strength, predictionResistance, pStr, OpenSsl.getInstance().dataParamBuffer(params));
+    }
+    void uninstantiate();
+    void generate(byte[] out, int strength, boolean predictionResistance,  byte[] addIn);
+    void reseed(boolean predictionResistance, byte[] entropy, byte[] addIn);
+    void enableLocking();
+    int strength();
+    State state();
+}

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl;
+
+/**
+ * Enum for supported named Elliptic Curves
+ */
+public enum EcCurve {
+    secp224r1("secp224r1", "1.3.132.0.33", 224),
+    secp256r1("prime256v1", "1.2.840.10045.3.1.7", 256), // NID_X9_62_prime256v1, secp256r1
+    secp384r1("secp384r1", "1.3.132.0.34", 384),
+    secp521r1("secp521r1", "1.3.132.0.35", 521);
+
+    private final String sn;
+    private final String oid;
+    private final int keyBits;
+
+    EcCurve(String sn, String oid, int keyBits) {
+        this.sn = sn;
+        this.oid = oid;
+        this.keyBits = keyBits;
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Gets the OpenSSL Short Name for the curve.
+     *  @return the curve OpenSSL Short Name as a String
+     */
+    public String sn() {
+        return this.sn;
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Gets the OID for the curve.
+     *  @return the curve OID as a String
+     */
+    public String oid() {
+        return this.oid;
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Returns the key bits strength associated with this curve.
+     *  @return the key bits of strength
+     */
+    public int keyBits() {
+        return this.keyBits;
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Returns the EcCurve that has the specified OpenSSL short name.
+     *  @param sn the OpenSSL short name of the curve to return
+     *  @return the EcCurve
+     */
+    public static EcCurve bySn(String sn) {
+        for (EcCurve c : values()) {
+            if (c.sn.equals(sn)) {
+                return c;
+            }
+        }
+        return null;
+    }
+}

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl;
+
+import java.security.ProviderException;
+
+/**
+ * Random byte generator.
+ */
+public final class Rand {
+
+    static private final int STRENGTH = 256;
+
+    public static byte[] generate(int numBytes) throws ProviderException {
+        byte[] bytes = new byte[numBytes];
+        generate(bytes);
+        return bytes;
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Generate random bytes.
+     * @param bytes the byte array to fill with random bytes
+     * @throws ProviderException if the bytes could not be generated
+     */
+    public static void generate(byte[] bytes) throws ProviderException {
+        LibCtx.randBytes(bytes, STRENGTH);
+    }
+
+    public void nextBytes(byte[] bytes) {
+        generate(bytes);
+    }
+}

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl.ffm;
+
+import java.lang.foreign.Arena;
+
+import com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl.OsslArena;
+
+final class ArenaImpl implements OsslArena {
+
+    static final OsslArena GLOBAL_INSTANCE = new ArenaImpl(Arena.global());
+
+    final Arena arena;
+
+    ArenaImpl(Arena arena) {
+        this.arena = arena;
+    }
+
+    public static OsslArena global() {
+        return GLOBAL_INSTANCE;
+    }
+
+    public static OsslArena ofAuto() {
+        return new ArenaImpl(Arena.ofAuto());
+    }
+
+    public static OsslArena ofConfined() {
+        return new ArenaImpl(Arena.ofConfined());
+    }
+
+    @Override
+    public void close() {
+        this.arena.close();
+    }
+}

+ * OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE
+ * SOFTWARE.

+
+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl.ffm;
+
+import java.lang.foreign.Arena;
+import java.lang.foreign.SymbolLookup;
+import java.nio.file.Path;
+
+import com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl.OpenSsl;
+
+public class OpenSslLoader {
+
+    static SymbolLookup libCrypto = null;
+    static boolean lifecycleHooksEnabled;
+
+    public static synchronized OpenSsl load(Path libCryptoPath, boolean enableLifecycleHooks) {
+        if (libCrypto != null) {
+            throw new IllegalStateException("OpenSsl already initialized");
+        }
+        libCrypto = SymbolLookup.libraryLookup(libCryptoPath, Arena.global());
+        lifecycleHooksEnabled = enableLifecycleHooks;
+
+        // Static initialization of the FfmOpenSsl class must not have already occurred prior to this point.
+        return new FfmOpenSsl();
+    }
+}

The values are determined at class-initialisation time based on + * the name and version of the OpenSSL FIPS provider loaded by Jipher at runtime. + * + *

The OpenSSL FIPS provider shipped with Linux distributions such as Oracle Linux + * does not support certain legacy algorithms; these are reported as not + * supported. + */ +public class Capabilities { + + private static final boolean DESEDE_IS_SUPPORTED; + private static final boolean DSA_IS_SUPPORTED; + private static final boolean SHA1_DIGEST_SIGNATURES_ARE_SUPPORTED; + + // Initialise capability flags based on the OpenSSL FIPS provider name and version. + static { + String name = FipsProviderInfo.getNameString(); + + boolean isRHDerivative = (name != null) && + (name.contains("Red Hat Enterprise Linux") || name.contains("Oracle Linux")); + + if (isRHDerivative) { + // For these Linux distributions, at the time of writing, only a single version, 3.0.7, + // of the OpenSSL FIPS module is distributed. +import java.math.BigInteger;
+import javax.crypto.spec.DHParameterSpec;
+import javax.crypto.spec.DHPrivateKeySpec;
+
+/**
+ * Extends the {@link DHPrivateKeySpec} class to also store
+ * the finite field domain parameters as a {@link DHParameterSpec}
+ */
+public class DHExtendedPrivateKeySpec extends DHPrivateKeySpec {
+    private final DHParameterSpec params;
+
+    /**
+     * Constructor that takes a private value {@code x} and DH parameter specification {@code params}.
+     * @param x the private value
+     * @param params the DH parameter spec
+     */
+    public DHExtendedPrivateKeySpec(BigInteger x, DHParameterSpec params) {
+        super(x, params.getP(), params.getG());
+        this.params = params;
+    }
+
+    /**
+     * Provides access to the DH parameters
+     *
+     * @return a {@link DHParameterSpec} contain the DH parameters.
+     */
+    public DHParameterSpec getParams() {
+        return params;
+    }
+}

+

+
+        assertArrayEquals(tv.getDigest(), digest);
+    }
+}

+package com.oracle.systest.serviceloader;
+
+import java.security.Provider;
+import java.util.Iterator;
+import java.util.ServiceLoader;
+
+import org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions;
+import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
+
+public class ServiceLoaderTest {
+    @Test
+    public void loadJipher()  {
+        // Iterate over Provider services known to the ServiceLoader looking for JipherJCE
+        Iterator<Provider> iter = ServiceLoader.load(java.security.Provider.class).iterator();
+        while (iter.hasNext()) {
+            try {
+                Provider provider = iter.next();
+                if (provider.getName().equals("JipherJCE")) {
+                    return; // Success
+                }
+            } catch (Exception ignored) {
+            }
+        }
+        Assertions.fail("Failed to load JipherJCE provider using ServiceLoader");
+    }
+}

+open module com.oracle.jiphertest.system {
+    // ClassLoader system tests do not list the Jipher JAR on the module path.
+    // Instead, they explicitly load the Jipher JAR at run time, from a system property path, using a ClassLoader.
+    // The 'static' directive, indicating the module is required and compile time but is optional at runtime,
+    // ensures the JVM will not throw:
+    //     java.lang.module.FindException: Module com.oracle.jipher not found, required by com.oracle.jiphertest.system
+    // on startup if the Jipher JAR is not listed on the module path
+    requires static com.oracle.jipher;
+
+    // ServiceLoader system tests use ServiceLoader to load java.security.Provider services
+    uses java.security.Provider;
+
+    requires com.oracle.jiphertest.other;
+
+    requires org.junit.jupiter.api;
+    requires junit; // Automatic module from junit:junit
+}

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.common;
+
+import java.security.InvalidKeyException;
+
+import org.junit.Test;
+
+public class TrippleDesUtilTest {
+
+    static final long WEAK_SUB_KEY = 0x0101010101010101L;
+
+    static final byte[] WEAK_KEY_MATERIAL =
+            new byte[]{0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01,
+                       0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01,
+                       0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01, 0x01};
+
+    @Test
+    public void isWeakKeyTest() {
+        TripleDesUtil.isWeakKey(new long[]{WEAK_SUB_KEY});
+    }
+
+    @Test(expected = InvalidKeyException.class)
+    public void createKeyNullKeyMataterialTest() throws Exception{
+        TripleDesUtil.createKey(null);
+    }
+
+    @Test(expected = InvalidKeyException.class)
+    public void createKeyInsufficientKeyMaterialTest() throws Exception{
+        TripleDesUtil.createKey(new byte[1]);
+    }
+
+    @Test(expected = InvalidKeyException.class)
+    public void createKeyWeakKeyMaterialTest() throws Exception{
+        TripleDesUtil.createKey(WEAK_KEY_MATERIAL);
+    }
+}

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.openssl;
+
+import org.junit.After;
+import org.junit.Assert;
+import org.junit.Before;
+
+public abstract class EvpTest {
+
+    OpenSsl openSsl;
+    OSSL_LIB_CTX libCtx;
+    OsslArena testArena;
+
+    @Before
+    public void setUp() throws Exception {
+        openSsl = OpenSsl.getInstance();
+        libCtx = LibCtx.getInstance();
+        testArena = OsslArena.ofConfined();
+    }
+
+    @After
+    public void tearDown() throws Exception {
+        testArena.close();
+    }
+
+    @After
+    public void verifyErrorQueueEmpty() {
+        // Detect if the test has left an error on the thread's OpenSSL error queue, which might indicate a bug
+        // either in the test or in the adapter layer.  It is important that errors inadvertently set in one
+        // test are not reported in another.
+        // Some OpenSSL API calls, in particular those on an uninitialised context, can add an error to the
+        // thread's OpenSSL error queue but not return an error return code from the API call.  As the API call
+        // does not return an error return code, an OpenSSL Exception, that would clear the thread's OpenSSL
+        // error queue, isn't built and thrown.
+        if (openSsl.peekLastError() != 0) {
+            StringBuilder exMsg = new StringBuilder("OpenSSL error queue:");
+            openSsl.forEachError(errorString -> exMsg.append('\n').append(errorString));
+            Assert.fail(exMsg.toString());
+        }
+    }
+}

+

+package com.oracle.jipher.internal.spi;
+
+import java.math.BigInteger;
+import javax.crypto.spec.DHParameterSpec;
+
+import org.junit.Test;
+
+import static org.junit.Assert.assertEquals;
+
+public class DHExtendedPrivateKeySpecTest {
+    @Test
+    public void constructGet() {
+
+        BigInteger a = BigInteger.valueOf(123);
+        BigInteger b = BigInteger.valueOf(456);
+        BigInteger c = BigInteger.valueOf(789);
+        DHParameterSpec d = new DHParameterSpec(b, c);
+
+        DHExtendedPrivateKeySpec spec = new DHExtendedPrivateKeySpec(a, d);
+
+        assertEquals(a, spec.getX());
+        assertEquals(b, spec.getP());
+        assertEquals(c, spec.getG());
+        assertEquals(d, spec.getParams());
+    }
+}

+def EMPTY_VERSION_RANGE = '(1.2.3, 1.2.3)'
+def OPENSSL_DEPENDENCIES = ['CryptoLibrary', 'FipsProvider']
+OPENSSL_DEPENDENCIES.each { dependency ->
+    tasks.register("unsanctioned${dependency}VersionTest", Test) {
+        description = "Run unsanctioned ${dependency} version test"
+        group = 'deploy-test'
+        useJUnitPlatform { includeEngines 'junit-jupiter' }
+        testClassesDirs = sourceSets.systemTest.output
+        classpath = sourceSets.systemTest.runtimeClasspath
+        forkEvery = 1
+
+        // Suppress restricted method warnings related to accessing methods considered to be unsafe
+        // See https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/25/core/restricted-methods.html
+        jvmArgs += '--enable-native-access=com.oracle.jipher'
+
+        def propertyDependencyName = dependency[0].toLowerCase() + dependency.substring(1)
+        systemProperty "jipher.openssl.sanctioned.${propertyDependencyName}Versions", EMPTY_VERSION_RANGE
+        systemProperty "jipher.openssl.sanctioned.osProvided.${propertyDependencyName}Versions", EMPTY_VERSION_RANGE
+
+        systemProperty 'debug.version.sanctioner', 'true'
+
+        include "com/oracle/systest/version/Unsanctioned${dependency}VersionTest.class"
+
+        finalizedBy 'testReport'
+    }
+}
+
+tasks.register('sanctionedVersionsTest') {
+    description = 'Run sanctioned version tests'
+    group = 'deploy-test'
+    OPENSSL_DEPENDENCIES.each { dependency ->
+        dependsOn "unsanctioned${dependency}VersionTest"
+    }
+}
+
+tasks.register('systemTest') {
+    description = 'Run system tests ( config tests and jar tests )'
+    group = 'deploy-test'
+    dependsOn 'fipsTest', 'configTest', 'jsseTest', 'classloaderTest', 'serviceloaderTest', 'sanctionedVersionsTest'
+}
+
+
+tasks.register('testReportAll', TestReport) {
+    destinationDirectory = file("$buildDir/reports/allTests")

+        println "Combined test results in:  $buildDir/reports/allTests/index.html"
+    }

+// Configure the TestReport task after all Test tasks have been created
+afterEvaluate {
+    tasks.named('testReportAll', TestReport) {
+        // Collect binary test results from all Test tasks
+        testResults.from(project.tasks.withType(Test).collect { it.binaryResultsDirectory })
+    }
+}
+
+tasks.register('testAll') {
+    description = 'Run full set of tests (system, integration)'
+    group = 'deploy-test'
+    dependsOn 'apiTest'
+    dependsOn 'apiAeadCipherTest_(stream)'
+    dependsOn 'systemTest'
+    finalizedBy 'testReportAll'
+}
+
+
+//
+// This below ensures that by default, when any testing tasks from this
+// system-test.gradle are executed (ie. group 'deploy-test'), all other tasks (such as compiling tasks)
+// are disabled. This is the behavior required for running test on remote machines.
+//
+// Disabled tasks can be re-enabled by supplying -PenableBuild=true property
+gradle.startParameter.taskNames.each { start ->
+    def fnd = project.getTasksByName(start, true).findAll { tsk -> tsk.group == 'deploy-test' }
+    if (!fnd.empty && project.ext.properties.enableBuild != 'true') {
+        gradle.taskGraph.whenReady { tg ->
+            tg.getAllTasks().each {
+                if (it.group != 'deploy-test') {
+                    it.enabled = false
+                }
+            }
+        }
+    }
+}

+apply from: 'jacoco.gradle'
+
+import java.nio.file.Files
+import java.nio.file.Paths
+
+project.ext.TEST_JAVA_VER = (project.findProperty('TEST_JAVA_VER')?.toInteger()) ?: project.ext.javaLanguageVersion
+
+project.ext.PLATFORM = determinePlatform()
+
+project.ext.STANDARD_JIPHER_NATIVE_DEPENDENCY_LOCATION = project.ext.PLATFORM.containsIgnoreCase('windows') ?
+        Paths.get('C:', 'Program Files', 'jipher') : Paths.get('/opt', 'jipher')
+project.ext.PREBUILT_JIPHER_NATIVE_DEPENDENCY_LOCATION =
+        Paths.get("${projectDir}", 'prebuilt', "${PLATFORM}")
+
+project.ext.STANDARD_JIPHER_OPENSSL_LOCATION = project.ext.STANDARD_JIPHER_NATIVE_DEPENDENCY_LOCATION.resolve('openssl')
+project.ext.PREBUILT_JIPHER_OPENSSL_LOCATION = project.ext.PREBUILT_JIPHER_NATIVE_DEPENDENCY_LOCATION.resolve('openssl')
+
+// Register the 'jipherTestJar' task so that it can be referenced in the sourceSets definitions.
+// The 'jipherTestJar' task will be configured after the sourceSets have been defined.
+tasks.register('jipherTestJar', Jar) {
+}
+
+sourceSets {
+    jipherTest {
+        // The jipherTest ProviderUtil creates a JipherJCE object
+        compileClasspath += sourceSets.main.output
+    }
+    test {
+        // tests depend on helpers, utilities and testdata defined in the jipherTest source set.
+        compileClasspath += sourceSets.jipherTest.output
+        runtimeClasspath += sourceSets.jipherTest.runtimeClasspath
+    }
+    intTest {
+        java {
+            srcDirs = ['src/intTest/java']
+            include 'com/oracle/test/integration/**'
+        }
+        resources.srcDir file('src/intTest/resources')
+
+        // Integration tests depend on helpers, utilities and testdata defined in the jipherTest source set.
+        compileClasspath += sourceSets.jipherTest.output
+        runtimeClasspath += sourceSets.jipherTest.runtimeClasspath
+
+        // Include the jipherTest JAR (which includes classes and resources) on the module path
+        // instead of the build/classes/java/jipherTest folder that only includes classes
+        runtimeClasspath -= sourceSets.jipherTest.output
+        runtimeClasspath += jipherTestJar.outputs.files
+
+        // Integration tests depend on the signed jipher-jce JAR instead of the jipher-jce build folder classes
+        compileClasspath += jar.outputs.files
+        runtimeClasspath += jar.outputs.files
+        runtimeClasspath -= sourceSets.main.runtimeClasspath
+    }
+    leakTest {
+        java.srcDir file('src/leakTest/java')
+
+        // Leak tests use an object leak monitor which accesses Jipher's OpenSSL interface
+        compileClasspath += sourceSets.main.output
+
+        // Leak tests depend on utilities defined in the jipherTest source set.
+        compileClasspath += sourceSets.jipherTest.output
+
+        // Leak Tests run the integration tests
+        compileClasspath += sourceSets.intTest.output
+        runtimeClasspath += sourceSets.intTest.runtimeClasspath
+        runtimeClasspath -= sourceSets.main.runtimeClasspath
+    }
+}
+
+// Configure the 'jipherTestJar' task
+tasks.named('jipherTestJar') {
+    group = "build"
+    description = "Assembles a jar archive containing classes that provide support for testing the library"
+
+    archiveClassifier = 'jipher-test'
+    from sourceSets.jipherTest.output
+}
+
+tasks.named('processJipherTestResources') {
+    duplicatesStrategy = DuplicatesStrategy.EXCLUDE
+}
+
+configurations {
+    jipherTestImplementation
+
+    intTestImplementation
+    intTestRuntimeOnly
+
+    leakTestImplementation.extendsFrom intTestImplementation
+}
+
+dependencies {
+    jipherTestImplementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.13.2'                 // testdata uses gson
+    jipherTestImplementation 'org.junit.vintage:junit-vintage-engine:5.14.3'    // Required to compile and run JUnit 4 Tests
+
+    testImplementation 'org.junit.vintage:junit-vintage-engine:5.14.3'          // Required to compile and run JUnit 4 Tests
+    testImplementation 'org.mockito:mockito-core:5.23.0'                        // Mocking depends on byte-buddy (see below)
+    testImplementation 'net.bytebuddy:byte-buddy:1.18.7'                        // ASM 9.9, which offers support for Java 26, was first included in 1.17.8
+    testRuntimeOnly 'org.junit.platform:junit-platform-launcher:1.14.3'         // Required to run JUnit 4 Tests
+
+    intTestImplementation 'org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter-api:5.14.3'          // Required to compile JUnit 5 Tests
+    intTestImplementation 'org.junit.vintage:junit-vintage-engine:5.14.3'       // Required to compile and run JUnit 4 Tests
+    intTestImplementation 'org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter-params:5.14.3'       // Required to compile and run JUnit 5 Parameterized Tests
+    intTestRuntimeOnly 'org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter-engine:5.14.3'          // Required to run JUnit 5 Tests
+    intTestRuntimeOnly 'org.junit.platform:junit-platform-suite-engine:1.14.3'  // Required to run JUnit 5 platform suites
+
+    leakTestImplementation 'org.junit.platform:junit-platform-suite-api:1.14.3' // Required to compile JUnit 5 platform suites
+}
+
+idea {
+    module {
+        testSources.from(project.sourceSets.intTest.java.srcDirs)
+    }
+}
+
+tasks.register('runMainClass', JavaExec) {
+    description = "Runs Jipher's main class. This can be used to verify that Jipher can locate OpenSSL runtime dependencies"
+    group = 'verification'
+
+    javaLauncher = javaToolchains.launcherFor {
+        languageVersion = JavaLanguageVersion.of(project.ext.TEST_JAVA_VER)
+    }
+
+    classpath = sourceSets.main.runtimeClasspath
+    mainClass = 'com.oracle.jipher.provider.JipherJCE'
+
+    // Suppress restricted method warnings related to accessing methods considered to be unsafe.
+    // See https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/25/core/restricted-methods.html
+    jvmArgs += '--enable-native-access=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    if (findProperty('jipher.openssl.dir') || findProperty('jipher.openssl.useOsInstance')) {
+        // Set a Java system property to locate OpenSSL if a gradle property of the same name is set
+        if (findProperty('jipher.openssl.dir')) {
+            systemProperty 'jipher.openssl.dir', findProperty('jipher.openssl.dir')
+        }
+        if (findProperty('jipher.openssl.useOsInstance')) {
+            systemProperty 'jipher.openssl.useOsInstance', findProperty('jipher.openssl.useOsInstance')
+        }
+    } else {
+        // If OpenSSL is not present in the standard jipher platform-specific location
+        if (!Files.exists(project.ext.STANDARD_JIPHER_OPENSSL_LOCATION)) {
+            // Attempt to set a java system property to locate OpenSSL in the prebuild folder
+            if (Files.exists(project.ext.PREBUILT_JIPHER_OPENSSL_LOCATION)) {
+                systemProperty 'jipher.openssl.dir', project.ext.PREBUILT_JIPHER_OPENSSL_LOCATION.toString()
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    // Capture stderr
+    errorOutput = new ByteArrayOutputStream()
+
+    doLast {
+        // Fail if captured output reports that an Exception was thrown
+        if (errorOutput.toString().contains("Exception")) {
+           throw new GradleException("runMainClass failed because stderr reported that an Exception was thrown:\n" +
+                   errorOutput.toString())
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+tasks.withType(Test) { task ->
+
+    javaLauncher = javaToolchains.launcherFor {
+        languageVersion = JavaLanguageVersion.of(project.ext.TEST_JAVA_VER)
+    }
+
+    jvmArgs += '-Xmx1024M'
+    // From JDK 9 onward internal JDK APIs are no longer accessible by default
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.internal.interfaces=ALL-UNNAMED'
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.internal.spec=ALL-UNNAMED'
+    // com.oracle.test.integration.tls.TlsTest uses sun.security.provider.X509Factory
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.provider=ALL-UNNAMED'
+    // Suppress restricted method warnings related to accessing methods considered to be unsafe.
+    // See https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/25/core/restricted-methods.html
+    jvmArgs += '--enable-native-access=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    // Suppress warning "Dynamic loading of agents will be disallowed by default in a future release"
+    // associated with mockito-inline dynamically loading byte-buddy-agent
+    jvmArgs += '-XX:+EnableDynamicAgentLoading'
+
+    // This restriction is required to ensure that the SessionTicketResumptionTest does not try to use x25519 or x448
+    jvmArgs += '-Djdk.tls.namedGroups=secp256r1,secp384r1,secp521r1,ffdhe2048,ffdhe3072,ffdhe4096,ffdhe6144,ffdhe8192'
+
+    // GSON, which is used by the testdata package in jiphertest module, uses sun.misc.Unsafe to create instances
+    // of classes it deserializes without calling any constructors
+    // Configure the JVM to resolve the jdk.unsupported module.
+    // See https://stackoverflow.com/questions/61727613/unexpected-behaviour-from-gson
+    // Avoids Caused by: java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException:
+    //        Cannot allocate class com.oracle.jiphertest.testdata.KeyPairTestData.
+    //        Usage of JDK sun.misc.Unsafe is enabled, but it could not be used. Make sure your runtime is configured correctly.
+    jvmArgs += '--add-modules'
+    jvmArgs += 'jdk.unsupported'
+
+    testLogging {
+        events 'failed'
+        showStackTraces = true
+    }
+    ['java.security.debug'].each {
+        if (System.getProperty(it) != null)
+            systemProperty it, System.getProperty(it)
+    }
+    outputs.upToDateWhen {false}
+    testLogging.showStandardStreams = true
+
+    addTestListener(new TestListener() {
+        void beforeTest(TestDescriptor testDescriptor) {}
+        void afterTest(TestDescriptor testDescriptor, TestResult result) {}
+        void beforeSuite(TestDescriptor suite) {}
+        void afterSuite(TestDescriptor descriptor, TestResult result) {
+            if (!descriptor.parent) {
+                def out = "Test Results: ${result.resultType} " +
+                        "(${result.testCount} tests, " +
+                        "${result.successfulTestCount} successes, " +
+                        "${result.failedTestCount} failures, " +
+                        "${result.skippedTestCount} skipped)"
+                println('\n' + ('='* out.length()) + '\n' + out + '\n' + ('='* out.length()))
+            }
+        }
+    })
+}
+
+tasks.withType(Test) {
+    if (findProperty('jipher.openssl.dir') || findProperty('jipher.openssl.useOsInstance')) {
+        if (findProperty('jipher.openssl.dir')) {
+            systemProperty 'jipher.openssl.dir', findProperty('jipher.openssl.dir')
+        }
+        if (findProperty('jipher.openssl.useOsInstance')) {
+            systemProperty 'jipher.openssl.useOsInstance', findProperty('jipher.openssl.useOsInstance')
+        }
+    } else {
+        if (!Files.exists(project.ext.STANDARD_JIPHER_OPENSSL_LOCATION)) {
+            // Attempt to set a system property to locate OpenSSL
+            if (Files.exists(project.ext.PREBUILT_JIPHER_OPENSSL_LOCATION)) {
+                systemProperty 'jipher.openssl.dir', project.ext.PREBUILT_JIPHER_OPENSSL_LOCATION.toString()
+            } else {
+                if (group != null) {
+                    println('WARNING: No OpenSSL location specified. Tests for group \'' + group + '\' will fail without it.')
+                    println('         (Specify an OpenSSL location by setting property jipher.openssl.dir or using the OS provided OpenSSL by setting the property jipher.openssl.useOsInstance=true.)')
+                }
+            }
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+test {
+    // White box test: Classes are listed on class path and thus module unnamed.
+
+    // From JDK 9 onward internal JDK APIs are no longer accessible by default
+    // com.oracle.jipher.internal.spi.InternalTlsSpecTest invokes methods on sun.security.internal.interfaces
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.internal.interfaces=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    // Jipher needs to access sun.security.internal.spec classes
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.internal.spec=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    // Suppress restricted method warnings related to accessing methods considered to be unsafe
+    // See https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/25/core/restricted-methods.html
+    jvmArgs += '--enable-native-access=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    gradle.taskGraph.whenReady {
+        if (it.hasTask(':sanity')) {
+            jacoco { enabled = true }
+        }
+        exclude '**/LifeCycleHookTest.class'
+    }
+    finalizedBy 'testReport'
+}
+
+tasks.register('lifeCycleHookTest', Test) {
+    description = 'Run the Life Cycle Hook tests with java.testing.lifecycleHooks.enable=true'
+    group = 'verification'
+    useJUnitPlatform { includeEngines 'junit-vintage' }
+    testClassesDirs = sourceSets.test.output
+    classpath = sourceSets.test.runtimeClasspath
+    gradle.taskGraph.whenReady {
+        if (it.hasTask(':sanity')) {
+            jacoco { enabled = true }
+        }
+        systemProperty 'java.testing.lifecycleHooks.enable', 'true'
+        include '**/LifeCycleHookTest.class'
+    }
+    finalizedBy 'testReport'
+}
+
+project.ext.commonIntegrationTestConfig =  {
+    // Black box test: Classes are listed on module path and thus are in named modules.
+
+    // From JDK 9 onward internal JDK APIs are no longer accessible by default
+    // com.oracle.test.integration.keygen.TlsMasterSecretKeyGenTest invokes methods on sun.security.internal.interfaces
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.internal.interfaces=com.oracle.jipher,com.oracle.jiphertest.integration'
+
+    // com.oracle.jiphertest.util.TlsUtil and com.oracle.test.integration.keygen.TlsKeyMaterialKeyGen*Test use specClass
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.internal.spec=com.oracle.jipher,com.oracle.jiphertest.other,com.oracle.jiphertest.integration'
+
+    // com.oracle.test.integration.tls.TlsTest uses sun.security.provider.X509Factory
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.provider=com.oracle.jiphertest.integration'
+
+    // Suppress restricted method warnings related to accessing methods considered to be unsafe
+    // See https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/25/core/restricted-methods.html
+    jvmArgs += '--enable-native-access=com.oracle.jipher'
+}
+
+tasks.register('integrationTest', Test) {
+    description = 'Runs integration tests.'
+    group = 'verification'
+    testClassesDirs = sourceSets.intTest.output.classesDirs
+    classpath = sourceSets.intTest.runtimeClasspath
+    configure project.ext.commonIntegrationTestConfig
+    useJUnitPlatform { includeEngines 'junit-jupiter', 'junit-vintage' }
+    mustRunAfter test
+    finalizedBy 'testReport'
+
+    exclude '**/*Suite.class'
+    if (TEST_JAVA_VER < 25) {
+        exclude '**/kdf/**'
+    }
+    gradle.taskGraph.whenReady {
+        if (it.hasTask(':sanity')) {
+            jacoco { enabled = true }
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+tasks.register('leakTest', Test) {
+    description = 'Runs object leak test suite.'
+    group = 'verification'
+    testClassesDirs = sourceSets.leakTest.output.classesDirs
+    classpath = sourceSets.leakTest.runtimeClasspath
+
+    // White box test: Classes are listed on class path and thus 'modules' are unnamed.
+
+    // From JDK 9 onward internal JDK APIs are no longer accessible by default
+    // com.oracle.test.integration.keygen.TlsMasterSecretKeyGenTest uses sun.security.internal.interfaces
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.internal.interfaces=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    // com.oracle.jiphertest.util.TlsUtil and com.oracle.test.integration.keygen.TlsKeyMaterialKeyGen*Test use sun.security.internal.spec
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.internal.spec=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    // com.oracle.test.integration.tls.TlsTest uses sun.security.provider.X509Factory
+    jvmArgs += '--add-exports=java.base/sun.security.provider=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    // Suppress restricted method warnings related to accessing methods considered to be unsafe
+    // See https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/25/core/restricted-methods.html
+    jvmArgs += '--enable-native-access=ALL-UNNAMED'
+
+    finalizedBy 'testReport'
+    useJUnitPlatform { includeEngines 'junit-platform-suite', 'junit-jupiter', 'junit-vintage' }
+    // Run the OpenSSL object leak test suite with native object lifecycleHooks enabled (required).
+    systemProperty 'java.testing.lifecycleHooks.enable', 'true'
+    include '**/OpenSslObjLeakSuite.class'
+}
+
+jacocoTestReport {
+    dependsOn lifeCycleHookTest, integrationTest // jacocoTestReport consumes the output of lifeCycleHookTest & integrationTest
+}
+
+tasks.register('sanityClean') {
+    dependsOn clean
+}
+// Ensure 'sanity' task  runs 'runMainClass' task before attempting to run any tests
+test.mustRunAfter('runMainClass')
+tasks.register('sanityRun') {
+    dependsOn runMainClass, build, jacocoTestReport, jacocoTestCoverageVerification
+    mustRunAfter 'sanityClean'
+}
+tasks.register('sanity') {
+    dependsOn sanityClean, sanityRun
+    description = 'Run sanity testing which builds jar and libraries and performs unit & integration testing with coverage.'
+    group = 'verification'
+}
+
+task testReport(type: TestReport) {
+    destinationDirectory = file("$buildDir/reports/allTests")
+    project.tasks.withType(Test) {
+        testResults.from(project.files("$buildDir/test-results/${it.name}/binary"))
+    }
+    doLast {
+        println "Combined test results in: $buildDir/reports/allTests/index.html"
+    }
+}
+
+def determinePlatform() {
+    if (System.properties['os.name'].toLowerCase().contains('linux'))
+        if (System.properties['os.arch'].toLowerCase().contains('aarch64'))
+            return 'linux_aarch64'
+        else
+            return 'linux_x64'
+    if (System.properties['os.name'].toLowerCase().contains('windows'))
+        return 'windows_x64'
+    if (System.properties['os.name'].toLowerCase().contains('mac'))
+        if (System.properties['os.arch'].toLowerCase().contains('aarch64'))
+            return 'macos_aarch64'
+        else
+            return 'macos_x64'
+    return 'unsupported platform'
+}
+
+apply from: 'system-test.gradle'
